title: Unable to build containers from working Dockerfile manifests on Mac using Linux remote host and ActiveDirectory Mac user
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Podman is being used on a Mac (`Darwin Kernel Version 19.6.0` or `Catalina 10.15.7`) pointing to a remote podman server running fedora configured over ssh. Pulling and running containers like `ubuntu` work just fine. But building a container with a Dockerfile that worked just fine in Docker-for-Mac does not. This is the first time I've used this setup where the front end was not pointing to the same machine as it's running on or in other words I have yet to test if the use of ActiveDirectory on this laptop is causing any issues and if the same issues would occur if using Docker with a remote host.

My user that I log into this laptop with is not found in `/etc/passwd` but is created by corporate IT via some AD software.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have a working ActiveDirectory user and be logged in as such on the Mac.

2. Setup podman to look at a remote host with the local host a Mac and the remote Linux (Fedora 34).

3. Try to build a simple hello-world Dockerfile.

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace ... UID:GID ... Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder130510716/build/.git: invalid argument`

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container to be built.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Wed Apr 21 20:33:43 2021
OS/Arch:      darwin/amd64

Server:
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Wed May 12 21:27:59 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman: stable 3.1.2 (bottled)
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Remote host running in Virtualbox with Fedora34 stock install.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out!

Can you look at /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid?  It looks like these files aren't set up yet (see http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman.1.html#rootless-mode).

@vrothberg It looks like he does - `podman info` shows a full swath of UIDs/GIDs available.

@gigatexal Can you provide the full error message? At the very least the UID/GID being requested as part of the failing `chown`? I suspect they are higher than 65536.

@mheon here's the full error:

```
podman build . -t gigatexal/podman:v.03
Error: potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 1526776272:20 for /var/tmp/libpod_builder186017614/build/.git): Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid: lchown /var/tmp/libpod_builder186017614/build/.git: invalid argument
```

1526776272 is the uid on my local machine
20 is the GID on my local machine for the current user

given the client/server nature of my setup the client being my mac and the server being a fedora vm i am not sure which end the fix needs to be created on

```
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      **host_id: 10**
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
```
This looks very strange, that your host_id in /etc/subgid is set to 10?
Good catch - that seems like a good candidate for the cause?

I've no idea how that's set to 10...

```
[gigatexal@container-vm ~]$ cat /etc/subuid
#gigatexal:100000:65536
gigatexal:1526776272:20
[gigatexal@container-vm ~]$ cat /etc/subgid
gigatexal:100000:65536
gigatexal:1526776272:20
```

is there a workaround?

```
cat /etc/subuid
#gigatexal:100000:65536
gigatexal:1526776272:20
```

This gives your container 20 UIDs?
it was a hack that I added as the previously commented out line didn't work. Should I change it to 10?
The default on Fedora is 65536. Can you uncomment the first line and remove the second one in both files? That should restore the defaults.

restored both files and still get the same error.

This could be something?

```
[gigatexal@container-vm ~]$ echo ${UID} ${GID} && whoami
1000
gigatexal
```

GID doesn't report anything for my user. 

```
[gigatexal@container-vm ~]$ cat /etc/passwd | grep gigatexal
gigatexal:x:1000:10::/home/gigatexal:/bin/bash
```

Can you run `podman system migrate`?  Maybe the user namespace is still open.

`1000:1000` is usually the default in `/etc/passwd`
sorry -- on the host or on the client with `--remote`?

ran it on the host, here's the output:

```

just wanted to thank everyone who's been helping me figure this out -- i know it's not very straightforward -- so thank you for your time.
closed as this is likely due to my wierd setup -- will use docker just in a custom linux vm with a the cli pointing to it instead of docker-for-mac
